items.summary: VideoLightbox uses a flat props interface (~40 individual props) while ImageLightbox groups them into sub-interfaces (navigation, shotWorkflow, features, actions). This inconsistency between the two sibling components makes the API harder to learn and maintain.

VisibilityManager is a fully implemented singleton (268 lines) that is never imported by any production code. Only referenced in browser-extensions.d.ts and its own test file. This is dead code that adds maintenance burden.

generationTaskBridge.ts defines GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping interfaces and the getTaskIdsForGenerations batch function that are only used internally within the file or in tests. enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData is only consumed by GenerationTaskContext (itself a wrapper). The batch function and types add abstraction without external consumers.

RawGeneration has a 'DEPRECATED' shot_generations field alongside the new shot_data JSONB format. The old join path is still actively used by 5+ files across the codebase, so this is not just a type definition issue but accumulated migration debt that was started and not finished.
PendingClipAction.type ('fill' | 'append') is set in consumePendingJoinClips but never read by applyPendingClipActions, which always tries empty-slot-first regardless of the type field.

The pattern of reading Supabase auth tokens from localStorage by parsing the project ref from SUPABASE_URL is duplicated in at least three files (taskCreation.ts, toolSettingsService.ts, invokeWithTimeout.ts) with slightly different shapes.
extractTaskId in generationTransformers declares it accepts `string[] | string | null` but silently drops a plain string value, returning null instead of the string itself.

GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping interfaces are defined but never used outside the file, and getTaskIdForGeneration/getTaskIdsForGenerations are private yet the comment block lists them as centralized consolidation targets.
fetchTaskContext in complete_task/index.ts types the supabase parameter as 'unknown' and then calls .from().select() on it, which bypasses all Supabase type checking at the edge function level.

The 'retro' and 'retro-secondary' button variants in button.tsx hardcode hex color values (e.g., #e8e4db, #3a4a4a, #6a8a8a) instead of using semantic CSS variables/Tailwind tokens. CLAUDE.md explicitly states 'never hardcode colors'.

Mixed usage of process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' and import.meta.env.DEV across shared code. In a Vite project, import.meta.env.DEV is the canonical check; process.env.NODE_ENV is not guaranteed to be available at runtime.

Edge functions use three different Deno std versions (0.168.0, 0.177.0, 0.224.0) and three different supabase-js import schemes (esm.sh@2.39.7, esm.sh@2, npm:@supabase/supabase-js@2). This creates risk of subtle runtime differences between functions.

useSettingsModal and useMediaGalleryHandlers use untyped CustomEvents on window ('openSettings', 'selectShotForAddition') to communicate across modules. These events bypass React's data flow, have no type-checked contracts, and create invisible coupling between distant components.

In useDeviceInfo, isTouchDevice is computed via useMemo depending only on [isMobile], but computeIsTouchDevice() reads window APIs directly and never uses isMobile. The value won't update when pointer capability changes unless isMobile also happens to change. The standalone useIsTouchDevice hook correctly uses resize listener but useDeviceInfo doesn't use it.

ShotActions accepts 22 individual props including multiple raw state setters and fine-grained boolean flags. This is a prop-drilling surface that makes the component hard to reason about and fragile to change.

The 'retro' and 'retro-secondary' button variants use extensive hardcoded hex colors (#e8e4db, #3a4a4a, #6a8a8a, etc.) instead of theme tokens, violating the project convention of never hardcoding colors.

ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx hardcodes Tailwind color values (bg-slate-50/50, dark:bg-slate-800/30) instead of using semantic design tokens (bg-background, bg-muted, etc.) as required by the project conventions.

The generateSummary callback in useAIInteractionService is a useCallback wrapper around generateSummaryForPromptInternal that adds no logic - it simply calls and returns the internal function. The comment 'Expose a version that uses the hook's API key' is stale since the API key is unused.

useAIInteractionService accepts an apiKey option that is immediately aliased to _apiKey (underscore-prefixed = unused). The comment says 'now strictly relies on this being passed' but the parameter is never used in any of the three edge function calls. This is a dead parameter that misleads callers.

useApiKeys.ts implements a manual check-then-insert-or-update pattern (lines 42-73) that could be replaced with a single Supabase upsert call, reducing code by roughly 15 lines and eliminating a race condition between the existence check and the insert/update.
The deprecated re-export shim at tools/edit-video/components/VideoPortionEditor.tsx still has one consumer (VideoReplaceMode.tsx) importing through it.
The deprecated shim at tools/travel-between-images/hooks/useSegmentOutputsForShot.ts still has 4 consumers importing through it instead of the canonical '@/shared/hooks/segments' path.
The deprecated re-export shim at shared/hooks/useSegmentSettings.ts still has one consumer (useSegmentSettingsForm.ts) importing from it via relative path instead of '@/shared/hooks/segments'.

useSegmentSettingsForm returns two deprecated fields (persistedEnhancePromptEnabled, saveEnhancePromptEnabled) alongside their replacements (effectiveEnhanceEnabled, handleEnhancePromptChange). The deprecated fields have zero external consumers.
useChangedDepsLogger computes a `changed` object at line 78-82 but never uses it (no console.log or return). The logging statement was removed but the computation was left.
useRenderLogger has an empty else-if branch at line 50-51. The periodic render-count log was removed but the conditional skeleton was left behind.
performanceUtils.ts is completely empty (only a doc comment, no exports). It has zero importers across the codebase and serves no purpose.
useInlineEditState accepts an _onClose parameter (prefixed with underscore indicating it's unused) but never uses it. This is a dead parameter in the public API.

createImageGenerationTask uses IIFEs (immediately-invoked function expressions) inside object spread syntax for conditional property inclusion. This creates hard-to-read nested closures, especially the LoRA merging at lines 326-342 and reference settings at lines 344-362.
LoRA validation logic (path required, strength 0-2) is duplicated verbatim between validateImageGenerationParams and validateBatchImageGenerationParams in imageGeneration.ts.

Error handling logic is scattered across four separate files at the same level in shared/lib: errorHandler.ts (re-export barrel), errors.ts (error classes), errorUtils.ts (type guards), and the errorHandling/ directory (handleError.ts, errorNormalization.ts, errorPresentation.ts). The barrel file errorHandler.ts re-exports from both errors.ts and errorHandling/handleError.ts, creating a confusing import graph where consumers use three different import paths for closely related functionality.

queryKeys.ts barrel file coexists alongside the queryKeys/ directory at the same level in shared/lib. The barrel re-exports all 12 sub-module key objects into a single queryKeys namespace, but consumers inconsistently import from either the barrel (queryKeys.ts) or the individual sub-modules (queryKeys/api.ts, etc.).

A README_timestamp_updates.md documentation file exists inside src/shared/hooks/, which is a code directory. This is a catalog/design doc that will drift from the actual implementation and violates the CLAUDE.md rule against creating catalog docs.

src/shared/utils/ directory still exists with 7 source files and a __tests__/ directory, despite the project description stating the straggler src/utils/ was deleted. This is a second utils directory that parallels shared/lib functionality.

Five test files in shared/lib sit alongside their source files rather than in the __tests__/ subdirectory that already exists in the same directory. This is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which uses __tests__/ directories.

Test files mock modules at wrong import paths, meaning the mocks do not actually intercept the real imports. useApiKeys.test.ts mocks '@/shared/lib/queryKeys' but the production code imports from '@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api'. useAIInteractionService.test.ts mocks '@/shared/lib/errorHandler' but the production code imports from '@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError'.

generateVideoService.test.ts contains a single trivial assertion (exports expected members) with `expect(true).not.toBe(false)` padding. The 650-line service with complex branching (timeline vs batch, phase config validation, structure guidance, pair overrides) has no meaningful test coverage.
timelineWriteQueue.test.ts is colocated with the source file instead of in a __tests__ directory, inconsistent with the project convention used by every other test file in the reviewed set.

All shared auth functions (authenticateRequest, verifyTaskOwnership, getTaskUserId, verifyShotOwnership) type the supabaseAdmin parameter as 'unknown', then call .from()/.select() on it without any type assertion or type guard. This erases all type checking on the Supabase client usage within auth.
The createGeneration helper function returns Promise<Record<string, unknown>> instead of the actual generation row type, discarding all type information about the returned database row.
GenerationTaskMapping and TaskGenerationMapping interfaces are defined but never used as type annotations anywhere in the module or its consumers.

The useToggleGenerationStar optimistic update casts shots cache data to Array<Record<string, unknown>> and nested images to Array<Record<string, unknown>>, losing all type safety on the Shot and GenerationRow structures being manipulated.

MediaGallery/types.ts defines MetadataLora locally with a comment 'Define types here to avoid circular imports' (line 11), but MetadataLora has zero external importers - it is only used internally within this file. The type and its comment appear to be scaffolding that was never wired up.
MediaGalleryProps.onPrefetchAdjacentPages is marked @deprecated (use generationFilters instead) but is still passed through in VideoTravelVideosGallery and MediaGallery's internal plumbing.

complete_task does not pass allowJwtUserAuth: true to authenticateRequest, while create-task and most other user-facing edge functions do. If a browser-based user sends a JWT to complete_task, it will fail authentication despite the user being valid.

complete_task does not handle CORS preflight (OPTIONS) requests or include CORS headers in responses, while create-task and delete-project do. If complete_task is ever called from a browser context, it will fail preflight.

usePaginatedTasks in useTasks.ts falls back to reading project ID from window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__, a global set by ProjectContext. This creates an implicit coupling outside the React tree and could cause stale reads.

useToggleGenerationStar uses window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('generation-star-updated')) to notify the Timeline of star updates. This is a side-channel that bypasses React's data flow, making it invisible to type checking and hard to trace.
The complete_task catch block returns internal error messages and partial stack traces to the caller, which can leak implementation details.
ShotActions uses hardcoded color classes (bg-green-500, bg-gray-500/60) for state indication instead of theme tokens.
useMediaGalleryHandlers uses a setTimeout(100ms) to sequence setting state and closing the lightbox, which is a timing-dependent hack that can race with React batching.

Multiple comments in useAIInteractionService.ts reference outdated implementation details ('new edge function', 'new implementation', 'the hook's API key') suggesting the code was migrated from a client-side AI API approach but the comments were not fully cleaned up.

ProjectImageSettings in ImageGenerationForm/types.ts has 7 deprecated fields (selectedLorasByTextModel, selectedReferenceId, styleReferenceImage, styleReferenceImageOriginal, styleReferenceStrength, subjectStrength, subjectDescription, inThisScene). Some are still read by migration/legacy code paths, but several appear to only exist in the type definition.

useDeviceInfo computes isTouchDevice via useMemo with [isMobile] as the only dependency, but computeIsTouchDevice reads from navigator and window which can change independently of isMobile (e.g., connecting a mouse to a tablet). The value may be stale.

In createGeneration, if the generation_variants insert fails, the error is logged but the function still returns the generation as if everything succeeded. The caller has no way to know the variant was not created.
usePaginatedTasks falls back to window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__?.selectedProjectId when projectId is not provided. This couples a hook to a hidden global, making data flow unclear and hard to trace.

MediaGallery passes an extremely large number of props to sub-components (MediaGalleryGrid receives ~50+ props). The component at 635 lines is essentially a prop-routing orchestrator, making it difficult to understand data flow.

generateClientThumbnail wraps callback-based Image/FileReader loading in a manual Promise constructor. This is functional but the nested callbacks (reader.onload -> img.onload -> canvas.toBlob) create deep nesting that is harder to follow.

generateVideoService.ts is 654 lines with deeply nested config-building functions. While it is already well-decomposed into helper functions, buildTravelRequestBody alone takes 24 parameters via a params object, making it fragile to extend.

useInlineEditState at 447 lines orchestrates 10+ hooks and manually threads individual properties between them via large builder functions (buildImageEditValueParams has 60+ lines of prop mapping, buildInlineEditStateResult has 60+ more). The resulting return type is a flat object with 50+ fields, making it fragile.
fetchLineageChain makes one Supabase query per ancestor in a while loop (N+1 pattern). For deep lineage chains this could produce many sequential round-trips.

useProcessingTimestamp.ts and useUpdatingTimestamp.ts both implement relative time formatting with similar patterns (minutes/hours/days with singular/plural) but use completely separate formatting logic. useProcessingTimestamp hand-rolls duration math while useUpdatingTimestamp wraps date-fns formatDistanceToNow with string replacements.

The pane-positioning/ subdirectory under shared/hooks uses kebab-case naming while all other subdirectories (gallery/, invalidation/, segments/, shots/, timeline/, etc.) use camelCase or single-word names. This is inconsistent with the recent renaming effort that converted kebab-case hook files to camelCase.

The placeholderData callback uses a non-standard function signature. React Query v5's placeholderData expects either a value or a function of (previousData, previousQuery), but the inline arrow uses only one parameter with an explicit type annotation that may conflict with the actual TanStack Query generics.

DataFreshnessManager shows a toast.success('Connection restored') on circuit breaker recovery, which contradicts the CLAUDE.md convention of 'Toasts: errors only (no success toasts for task creation)'.

InstallInstructionsModal's browser mockup components use ~91 instances of hardcoded gray/color classes (bg-gray-100, bg-gray-800, text-gray-400, bg-red-400, bg-yellow-400, bg-green-400, bg-blue-500, etc.) rather than theme tokens.

useTasks.ts uses both 'import React from react' and destructured hooks pattern inconsistently. React.useRef and React.useEffect are used on lines 352-358 instead of destructured imports, while the file also uses named imports from react-query.
Minor quality variations in naming quality

Three debug-related files (debugConfig.ts, debugPolling.ts, debugRendering.ts) sit at the shared/lib/ root level rather than in a debug/ subdirectory, mirroring the error handling fragmentation pattern.
supabaseTypeHelpers.ts has no test file. While the function is trivial (a type cast), it represents an explicit boundary cast that the module comment says should be trackable.

narrative.headline: (70 review findings — run `desloppify issues`)

actions.description: 70 review findings need investigation — run `desloppify issues` to see the work queue
4 unused findings — run `desloppify fix unused-imports --dry-run` to preview, then apply
1 orphaned findings — delete dead files or relocate with `desloppify move`
20 flat_dirs findings — create subdirectories and use `desloppify move`
1 naming findings — rename files with `desloppify move` to fix conventions
1 cycles findings — break cycles by extracting shared code or using `desloppify move`
1 facade findings — flatten re-export facades or consolidate barrel files
82 structural findings — decompose large files — extract logic into focused modules
125 props findings — split bloated components, extract sub-components
14 react findings — refactor React antipatterns (state sync, provider nesting, hook bloat)
2 dupes findings — extract shared utility or consolidate duplicates
2 patterns findings — align to single pattern across the codebase
4 test_coverage findings — add tests for untested production modules — prioritize by import count
16 signature findings — consolidate inconsistent function signatures
10 responsibility_cohesion findings — split modules with too many responsibilities — extract focused sub-modules
648 boilerplate_duplication findings — extract shared boilerplate into reusable helpers or base classes
175 smells findings — inspect with `desloppify next` and fix manually
2 deprecated findings — remove deprecated symbols or migrate callers
662 files need design review — run design review with dimension templates

strategy.hint: 5 independent workstreams, safe to parallelize. Rescan after each phase to verify. Review: 70 finding(s) — `desloppify issues`.

move.reason: 1 orphaned files + 20 flat directories + 1 naming issues

primary_action.description: 70 review findings need investigation — run `desloppify issues` to see the work queue

narrative.why_now: 5 independent workstreams, safe to parallelize. Rescan after each phase to verify. Review: 70 finding(s) — `desloppify issues`.
